calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-28-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian McCaffrey for Connie Roberts (Personal Representative now deceased). Shashauna Szczechowicz for American Contractors Indemnity Company (Bonding Company). Brett Lytle for Pamela Pullen (Beneficiary).

Mr. McCaffrey stated that Mr. Smith and his Client knew about the loan, that it was a hard loan and that it was the only way to transfer the property to Larry without the property being reassessed. Mr. Lytle stated that the $14,000.00 amount resolved all of their discussion. Ms. Szczechowicz stated that she has nothing to add as long as the order shows a liability of the Bonding Company as $11,116.03. The Court granted the accounting petition (subject to receipt and review of a revised order). Mr. McCaffrey is to revise the order which is to include/address the surcharge issue and is to be circulated amongst Counsel.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-31-2012 Continued To Aug-28-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Shashauna Szczechowicz for American Contractors Indemnity Company and also making special appearances for Brian McCaffrey Counsel for Connie Roberts and for Brett Lytle Counsel for Pamela Pullen.

Ms. Szczechowicz stated that she filed a stipulation yesterday to continue the matter, and that she believes that they can resolve the accounting and surcharge issues. The accounting petition was continued to August 28, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy C. Garcia.

Off Calendar May-01-2012 Continued To Jul-10-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian McCaffrey for Connie Roberts (Deceased). Shashauna Szczechowicz for American Contractors Indemnity Company. Brett Lytle for Pamela Pullen (Beneficiary).

Mr. McCaffrey stated that Connie did not waive fees in writing and that it was his understanding that she did not intend to request fees. Ms. Szczechowicz requested a 60-day continuance to see where the funds are. The Accounting Petition was continued to July 10, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Off Calendar Dec-14-2011 Continued To Jan-25-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian McCaffrey for Connie Roberts.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2
Off Calendar Feb-02-2009 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2
Off Calendar Dec-08-2008 Continued To Feb-02-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian McCaffrey for Petitioner.

Mr. McCaffrey stated that Larry Smith signed the consent, that Ms. Pullen's consent was in the mail, and requested a continuance. In addition, he stated that Frank Yanushefsky (Surviving Spouse) was deceased and that he was informed they were divorced. The Court requested the date of death (ideally a death certificate) and a divorce decree. The Court also requested that Counsel file an "Amendment" to the Amended Second Account spelling out the terms of the two loans, specifics as to the where the proceeds of the $135,000.00 loan are being held (and its history), and submission of the original bank statements with the ending balances. The Court admonished Counsel regarding responding promptly to the Examiner's notes and stated that everything was to be done by the continued date of February 2, 2009.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: J. Alameda./R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.
